events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes an HVAC-R residential job where the thermostat/sequence work has been tested/inspected, and the field condition is "aux heat locked on." This means the auxiliary heat is stuck on, which is a defect in the thermostat or control sequence. This is a malfunction — the aux heat should only come on when needed (e.g., when the heat pump can't keep up). If it's locked on, it's an installed-defective condition, non-compliant with manufacturer instructions, and wastes energy / could cause high bills or overheating. Decision: fail. Risk: medium-high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 90 (tested/inspected). Value: 0 (not asked). Confidence high. Findings and actions accordingly.
